title: plantago juncoides var. glauca
intro: Plantago juncoides var. glauca, also known as Sea Plantain, is a perennial plant species in the Plantaginaceae family, characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small white flowers. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Sea Plantain include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce swelling, and support wound healing. The herbal preparation of Sea Plantain can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat minor cuts and scrapes, as well as digestive problems such as diarrhea and ulcers.
